_about: Curated, citation-tagged excerpts of the actual 27 CFR sections used by the RAG layer (Layer 3). Each chunk is one citable unit; metadata IS the citation. §16.21 is verbatim (the official statement); the others are faithful, conservative statements of the cited section's requirement. Covers the health warning (Part 16) and the per-commodity labeling rules: wine (Part 4), distilled spirits (Part 5), and malt beverages (Part 7). This committed corpus keeps the prototype fully OFFLINE and reproducible; the full live eCFR ingest (all of Parts 4/5/7/16 + the BAM) is the deferred build-time step. All section NUMBERS were verified against the live eCFR structure API for title 27 (issue date 2026-06-10): wine §4.25/§4.32/§4.34/§4.36, spirits §5.63/§5.65/§5.74, malt §7.63/§7.65, warning §16.20/§16.21/§16.22. The 'heading' field is a descriptive label for retrieval, not the verbatim eCFR section title (e.g. eCFR §16.21 is titled 'Mandatory label information' but contains the GOVERNMENT WARNING text). Effective dates remain representative; confirm exact wording against the source_url before operational use.
_retrieved: 2026-06-18; section numbers verified against eCFR title-27 structure (issue date 2026-06-10)

chunks.heading: Health warning statement requirement
chunks.text: Any person who manufactures, imports, or bottles alcoholic beverages for sale or distribution in the United States must ensure that the container bears the Government health warning statement set out in 27 CFR 16.21. The requirement applies to all alcoholic beverages, including wine, distilled spirits, and malt beverages.

chunks.heading: The Government Warning statement (verbatim)
chunks.text: GOVERNMENT WARNING: (1) According to the Surgeon General, women should not drink alcoholic beverages during pregnancy because of the risk of birth defects. (2) Consumption of alcoholic beverages impairs your ability to drive a car or operate machinery, and may cause health problems.

chunks.heading: Legibility and format of the warning
chunks.text: The health warning statement must be readily legible, appear on a contrasting background, and be separate and apart from other information. The words 'GOVERNMENT WARNING' must appear in capital letters and in bold type. The remainder of the statement may not appear in bold.

chunks.heading: Mandatory label information for wine
chunks.text: Labels of wine must state: the brand name; the class, type, or other designation; the alcohol content; the name and address of the bottler or importer; and the net contents. The container must also bear the Government health warning statement required by 27 CFR part 16.

chunks.heading: Class and type designation for wine
chunks.text: The class and type of a wine must be stated in accordance with the standards of identity (for example, 'table wine', 'red wine', a grape varietal designation, or a recognized appellation of origin). The designation must be conspicuous and not misleading.

chunks.heading: Alcohol content statement for wine
chunks.text: Alcohol content for wine must be stated as a percentage of alcohol by volume. Table wine may instead be labeled 'table wine' or 'light wine' without a numerical alcohol statement. Tolerances apply to the stated percentage.

chunks.heading: Appellation of origin for wine
chunks.text: An appellation of origin (such as a country, state, county, or approved viticultural area) may appear on a wine label only if the stated percentage of the wine was produced from grapes grown in the named place, in accordance with the applicable percentage requirements.

chunks.heading: Mandatory label information for distilled spirits
chunks.text: Labels of distilled spirits must state: the brand name; the class and type or other designation; the alcohol content; the name and address of the bottler or importer; the net contents; and, where applicable, a commodity statement. The container must also bear the Government health warning statement required by 27 CFR part 16.

chunks.heading: Alcohol content statement for distilled spirits
chunks.text: Alcohol content for distilled spirits must be stated as a percentage of alcohol by volume. The equivalent proof (two times the percentage of alcohol by volume) may also be shown in direct conjunction with the percentage statement. This requirement applies to all distilled spirits, including vodka, whisky, rum, gin, brandy, and tequila.

chunks.heading: Statements of age, storage, and percentage for distilled spirits
chunks.text: Age is the length of time, after distillation and before bottling, that distilled spirits have been stored in oak barrels. An age statement is required on the label of whisky (and on brandy, except for immature types) that is less than four years old, and is optional otherwise. When an age is stated, it must be the age of the youngest spirit in the product. Age, storage, and maturity representations must not be misleading.

chunks.heading: Mandatory label information for malt beverages
chunks.text: Labels of malt beverages must state: the brand name; the class and type or other designation (for example, 'beer', 'ale', 'lager', or 'malt liquor'); the name and address of the brewer, bottler, or importer; and the net contents. The container must also bear the Government health warning statement required by 27 CFR part 16.

chunks.heading: Alcohol content statement for malt beverages
chunks.text: When alcohol content is stated on a malt beverage label, it must be expressed as a percentage of alcohol by volume. An alcohol content statement is mandatory where required by State law and is otherwise optional, except that it must appear when the malt beverage is labeled with certain strength or potency representations.
